description_long: This is a regional passenger airplane with a sleek, elongated fuselage painted primarily white and adorned with blue airline branding and logos. The aircraft features a high-wing configuration with straight wings, each equipped with a turboprop engine and four-bladed propellers mounted on nacelles. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with visible main gear under the wings and a nose gear beneath the cockpit. The airplane's surface is smooth and glossy, with distinct panel lines and access doors visible along the fuselage.
description: A white, high-wing regional turboprop airplane with blue airline branding, twin propeller engines, and a conventional tail. The aircraft has a sleek, smooth fuselage and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, high-mounted wings with turboprop engines, and airline branding along the body.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, propellers, and the symmetrical high-wing configuration.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the fuselage, wing, and tail, with clear visibility of the propeller and airline logo on the tail.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the straight wingspan, engine placement, and the overall planform of the airplane.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets; engines mounted on the wings with slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows for pilot visibility.
